page: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor clocked at 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 128 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 64 KB L1d cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography enabled. It does not support hyperthreading, meaning each of the 48 vCPUs maps to a dedicated physical core. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D48pls_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core execution, and Passmark CPU physics testing all rank in the top 10% tier. Memory performance is a notable strength, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 463.86 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 199.92 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10%. Single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and Passmark CPU scores falling into the middle 50% average category. For specialized tasks, the server achieves strong top-25% marks in Redis SET operations (89.58 million operations per second) and static web serving throughput (76.06 GB/sec). Large-scale LLM inference presents a performance bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model dropping to a weak 6.91 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48pls_v6 offers high compute density and memory bandwidth relative to its resource profile, making it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the low memory allocation of 2.0 GB per core represents a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. This server is suited for multi-threaded CPU-bound workloads, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale text generation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an arm64-based compute-optimized virtual machine powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading, 96.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core), and no local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by its top 10% rankings in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU physics tests. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 463.86 GB/sec for large-block reads. However, single-core benchmarks and large-scale LLM inference (70B model) show average to weak performance. Given its low memory-to-core ratio, the D48pls_v6 is best suited for multi-threaded CPU-bound tasks, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving rather than memory-heavy or large-scale AI workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an arm64 virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 96 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an arm64 VM with 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores and 96 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized virtual machine delivering high multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth for dedicated parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 cores at 3.4 GHz.
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 463.86 GB/sec large-block read speeds.
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations.
Lacks local storage and integrated GPU accelerators.
Best suited for static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-bound tasks.
